name: Conversation Engine
description: Core conversation loop orchestrating the three-phase interview (understand → challenge → synthesize). Uses synchronous anthropic.Anthropic() client with claude-sonnet-4-20250514 for all LLM calls. Manages phase transitions, turn management with structured JSON parsing, round enforcement with configurable min/max limits, two-tier retry (API-level exponential backoff + parse-level re-prompting), interrupt handling (Ctrl+C saves session, Ctrl+D advances phase), synthesis delegation with feedback loop, and session resume support. All terminal I/O is abstracted behind a TerminalIO protocol for testability.

types.name: Phase
types.description: The three phases of the constrain interview process, executed in sequence.

types.name: PhaseConfig

fields.name: phase
fields.description: Which phase this config applies to.

fields.name: min_rounds
fields.description: Minimum number of conversation rounds before ready_to_proceed is honored. Below this, ready_to_proceed=true is ignored.

fields.name: max_rounds
fields.description: Maximum number of rounds before forced phase transition. At this count, phase ends regardless of ready_to_proceed.

fields.name: posture_id
fields.description: Identifier passed to posture module to retrieve the appropriate system prompt for this phase. Must not be revealed to the engineer.

types.description: Declarative configuration for a single interview phase, controlling round enforcement boundaries and the posture identifier used for system prompt generation.

types.name: Message

fields.name: role
fields.description: Message role: 'user' or 'assistant'.

fields.name: content
fields.description: Full text content of the message. For assistant messages, includes both natural language and any JSON blocks.

types.description: A single message in the conversation history. Stores full LLM responses including JSON blocks. Role is 'user' for engineer input or 'assistant' for LLM output.

types.name: MessageHistory
types.description: Ordered list of conversation messages. System prompt is NOT stored here; it is injected per-API-call from the posture module. Full LLM responses (including JSON blocks) are preserved.

types.name: TurnResult

fields.name: display_text
fields.description: The natural language portion of the LLM response, with JSON blocks stripped. This is what gets shown to the engineer.

fields.name: model_update
fields.description: Incremental updates to the problem model parsed from the structured JSON block. Keys are ProblemModel field names, values are the updates. Empty dict if no updates.

fields.name: ready_to_proceed
fields.description: Whether the LLM indicates the current phase is complete. Subject to round enforcement: ignored if below min_rounds, overridden to true at max_rounds.

fields.name: raw_response
fields.description: The complete unmodified LLM response text, including JSON blocks. Stored in message history for full fidelity.

types.description: Structured result from executing a single conversation turn. Cleanly separates the parsed components of an LLM response for the orchestrator.

types.name: PhaseResult

fields.name: phase
fields.description: Which phase produced this result.

fields.name: messages
fields.description: All messages exchanged during this phase (does not include prior phases).

fields.name: rounds_completed
fields.description: Number of full conversation rounds (user + assistant pairs) completed in this phase.

fields.name: exit_reason
fields.description: How and why the phase ended.

fields.name: problem_model_snapshot
fields.description: Serialized ProblemModel state at the end of the phase. Represents cumulative updates from all turns.

types.description: Result of running a single phase to completion. Contains the updated problem model, conversation transcript for the phase, final round count, and how the phase ended.

types.name: PhaseExitReason
types.description: Describes how a phase ended, used for logging and session state.

types.name: SynthesisContext

fields.name: problem_model
fields.description: Serialized ProblemModel containing all discovered problem structure from understand and challenge phases.

fields.name: conversation_transcript
fields.description: Full conversation history across all phases, providing rich context for artifact generation.

fields.name: session_id
fields.description: Session identifier for traceability in generated artifacts.

types.description: All context needed by the Synthesizer to generate artifacts. Assembled by the engine from accumulated session state.

types.name: SynthesisResult

fields.name: prompt_md
fields.description: Generated prompt.md content — the induced-understanding prompt artifact.

fields.name: constraints_yaml
fields.description: Generated constraints.yaml content — the structured constraint set artifact.

fields.name: feedback_rounds
fields.description: Number of feedback/regeneration rounds that occurred (0 if accepted immediately, 1 if regenerated once).

types.description: Output of the synthesis phase. Contains generated artifact content ready for file writing.

types.name: TerminalIO

fields.name: display
fields.description: Callable[[str], None] — Display text to the engineer. Handles newlines and formatting.

fields.name: prompt
fields.description: Callable[[str], str] — Prompt the engineer for input with the given prefix string. Returns the input string. Raises EOFError on Ctrl+D.

types.description: Protocol (structural typing) for terminal input/output. Production implementation wraps print() and input() with readline support. Test implementations inject mock I/O. Implementations must provide display() and prompt() methods.

types.name: EngineConfig

fields.name: understand_min_rounds
fields.description: Minimum rounds for understand phase.

fields.name: understand_max_rounds
fields.description: Maximum rounds for understand phase.

fields.name: challenge_min_rounds
fields.description: Minimum rounds for challenge phase.

fields.name: challenge_max_rounds
fields.description: Maximum rounds for challenge phase.

fields.name: model_name
fields.description: Claude model identifier for all API calls.

fields.name: api_max_retries
fields.description: Maximum API call retry attempts on transient errors (rate limit, timeout, connection).

fields.name: parse_max_retries
fields.description: Maximum re-prompt retries when JSON parse fails from LLM response.

types.description: Top-level configuration for the ConversationEngine, combining phase configs and operational settings. Supports CLI flag and env var overrides.

types.name: ParsedResponseBlock

fields.name: display_text
fields.description: LLM response with the last ```json ... ``` block removed.

fields.name: json_data
fields.description: Parsed contents of the last ```json ... ``` fenced block. Contains ready_to_proceed and problem_model_update keys.

types.description: Result of parsing an LLM response to extract the structured JSON block and natural language text. Output of the pure _parse_response function.

types.name: ApiCallParams

fields.name: system_prompt
fields.description: System prompt for this call, generated by posture module based on current phase.

fields.name: messages
fields.description: Conversation message history to send.

fields.name: model
fields.description: Model identifier (e.g., claude-sonnet-4-20250514).

types.description: Parameters for a single API call to the Anthropic client. Encapsulates everything needed for the _call_api method.

types.name: EngineError
types.description: Categories of errors the engine can produce.

functions.name: __init__
functions.description: Construct a ConversationEngine with all injectable dependencies. Builds PhaseConfig instances from EngineConfig. Validates that min_rounds <= max_rounds for each phase. Does NOT start any conversation — call run_session() or run_phase() after construction.

inputs.name: client
inputs.description: Synchronous anthropic.Anthropic() client instance. Must be pre-configured with API key.

inputs.name: session
inputs.description: Session model instance (from models module). May be a fresh or resumed session. Engine reads and mutates session state.

inputs.name: io
inputs.description: Terminal I/O implementation. Production uses real stdin/stdout with readline. Tests inject mock.

inputs.name: config
inputs.description: Engine configuration including round limits and retry settings.

error_cases.name: invalid_phase_config
error_cases.condition: min_rounds > max_rounds for any phase

error_data.message: min_rounds must be <= max_rounds for each phase

preconditions: client is a valid synchronous anthropic.Anthropic instance with a configured API key
session is a valid Session model instance from models module
io implements the TerminalIO protocol (has display and prompt methods)
config.understand_min_rounds <= config.understand_max_rounds
config.challenge_min_rounds <= config.challenge_max_rounds
postconditions: Engine is ready to run via run_session() or run_phase()
PhaseConfig instances are constructed from EngineConfig values
No API calls or I/O have been performed

functions.name: run_session
functions.description: Main orchestrator. Runs all remaining phases in sequence (understand → challenge → synthesize), managing phase transitions with terminal indicators (e.g., '--- Moving to challenge phase ---'). For resumed sessions, checks session state for current phase and round, skips completed phases, displays a resume summary via IO, and continues from the saved point. Catches KeyboardInterrupt at this level to save session state and exit gracefully. EOFError is handled per-phase within run_phase(). Returns the completed session with all artifacts populated.

error_cases.name: keyboard_interrupt
error_cases.condition: Engineer presses Ctrl+C at any point during the session

error_cases.name: api_exhausted_fatal
error_cases.condition: API retry exhausted during any phase and session cannot continue

error_data.message: API unavailable after retries. Session saved.

error_cases.name: session_save_failure
error_cases.condition: Failed to save session state on exit

error_data.message: WARNING: Failed to save session state.

preconditions: Engine has been constructed via __init__ with valid dependencies
Session is in a valid state (fresh or partially completed)
postconditions: All remaining phases have been executed to completion (or interrupted)
Session state reflects final phase, round, problem model, and artifacts
If synthesis completed, session contains prompt_md and constraints_yaml
Session is saved via session manager on both success and interrupt
Phase transition indicators have been displayed to the engineer
side_effects: Displays conversation output and phase transitions to terminal via IO
Reads engineer input from terminal via IO
Makes multiple synchronous API calls to Anthropic Claude
Saves session state to disk via session manager
Delegates to synthesizer for artifact generation

structured_side_effects.description: Multiple synchronous messages.create calls across all phases
Session state persisted on completion and on interrupt
Session phase, round, problem_model, messages, and artifacts are updated throughout
Displays LLM responses, phase transitions, resume summary, and error messages

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is total rounds across all phases

functions.name: run_phase
functions.description: Execute a single interview phase to completion. Loops: get system prompt from posture, send messages to Claude via _call_api, parse response via _execute_turn, display natural language to engineer, collect engineer input, enforce round limits. Below min_rounds, ready_to_proceed=true from LLM is ignored. At max_rounds, phase transitions regardless. Catches EOFError (Ctrl+D) at this level: in understand/challenge, it advances to the next phase; in synthesize, it accepts current artifacts as-is. Updates session state after each turn.

error_cases.name: api_exhausted
error_cases.condition: All API retry attempts exhausted for a turn in this phase

error_data.message: Claude API unavailable after max retries

error_cases.name: parse_exhausted
error_cases.condition: JSON parse retry exhausted — LLM consistently returns malformed structured blocks

error_data.message: Failed to parse structured response after retries

error_cases.name: phase_already_completed
error_cases.condition: Session state indicates this phase was already completed

error_data.message: Phase already completed in this session

preconditions: Engine has been constructed via __init__
The specified phase has not already been completed in the session
For CHALLENGE: UNDERSTAND phase must be completed
For SYNTHESIZE: UNDERSTAND and CHALLENGE phases must be completed
postconditions: PhaseResult contains all messages exchanged during the phase
PhaseResult.rounds_completed reflects actual rounds completed
PhaseResult.exit_reason accurately describes how the phase ended
Session state has been updated with phase results
If phase is UNDERSTAND or CHALLENGE, problem_model_snapshot reflects cumulative updates
If exit_reason is EOF_ADVANCE, the phase ended early but cleanly
side_effects: Displays LLM responses and prompts engineer for input via IO
Makes API calls to Claude for each turn
Mutates session state with conversation history and problem model updates

structured_side_effects.description: One API call per turn plus potential retries
Phase progress, messages, problem_model updated per turn
Displays assistant responses and reads engineer input

functions.name: _execute_turn
functions.description: Execute a single conversation turn: call the API, parse the response (with parse-level retry on failure), and return a structured TurnResult. On JSON parse failure, re-prompts the LLM with format correction instructions up to parse_max_retries times. Appends the assistant's raw response (including JSON) to the message history. This is the core turn-level method that combines API calling and response parsing with retry logic.

inputs.name: system_prompt
inputs.description: Phase-appropriate system prompt from posture module.

inputs.name: messages
inputs.description: Current conversation history including the latest user message.

error_cases.name: api_exhausted
error_cases.condition: API call failed after all retry attempts

error_cases.name: parse_exhausted
error_cases.condition: JSON parse failed after initial attempt + parse_max_retries re-prompts

preconditions: system_prompt is non-empty
messages is non-empty and last message has role 'user'
API client is configured and available
postconditions: TurnResult.display_text contains only natural language (no JSON blocks)
TurnResult.model_update is a valid dict (may be empty)
TurnResult.raw_response is the complete unmodified LLM response
TurnResult.ready_to_proceed reflects the LLM's assessment (before round enforcement)

structured_side_effects.description: Appends assistant response(s) and retry prompts to message history

performance_budget.complexity: O(1) per turn, O(parse_max_retries) worst case

functions.name: _call_api
functions.description: Make a single synchronous API call to Claude with exponential backoff retry on transient errors (RateLimitError, APITimeoutError, APIConnectionError). Uses the configured model name. Returns the raw text content from the response. Backoff schedule: 1s, 2s, 4s (doubling). Only retries on transient/retriable errors; auth errors and other permanent failures raise immediately.

inputs.name: params
inputs.description: Encapsulated API call parameters: system prompt, messages, and model name.

error_cases.name: rate_limit_exhausted
error_cases.condition: RateLimitError persists after api_max_retries attempts with exponential backoff

error_cases.name: timeout_exhausted
error_cases.condition: APITimeoutError persists after api_max_retries attempts

error_cases.name: connection_exhausted
error_cases.condition: APIConnectionError persists after api_max_retries attempts

error_cases.name: auth_error
error_cases.condition: Authentication or authorization error from Anthropic API (not retried)

preconditions: params.system_prompt is non-empty
params.messages is non-empty
params.model is a valid Claude model identifier
Anthropic client is configured with valid API key
postconditions: Return value is the text content from Claude's response
Return value is non-empty
If retries occurred, appropriate backoff delays were observed

structured_side_effects.description: 1 to api_max_retries synchronous HTTP calls with exponential backoff

functions.name: _parse_response
functions.description: Pure function. Parse an LLM response string to extract the structured JSON block and natural language text. Finds the LAST ```json ... ``` fenced code block using regex. Extracts and parses the JSON, expecting keys 'ready_to_proceed' (bool) and 'problem_model_update' (dict). Returns the display text (response with JSON block removed) and parsed data. Raises ValueError on parse failure — caller handles retry logic.

inputs.name: raw_response
inputs.description: Complete raw LLM response text potentially containing a ```json ... ``` block.

error_cases.name: no_json_block
error_cases.condition: No ```json ... ``` fenced block found in response

error_data.message: No JSON code block found in LLM response

error_cases.name: invalid_json
error_cases.condition: JSON block contents are not valid JSON

error_data.message: Invalid JSON in response code block

error_cases.name: missing_required_keys
error_cases.condition: Parsed JSON lacks 'ready_to_proceed' or 'problem_model_update' keys

error_data.message: JSON block missing required keys: ready_to_proceed, problem_model_update

error_cases.name: wrong_types
error_cases.condition: ready_to_proceed is not bool or problem_model_update is not dict

error_data.message: JSON block has incorrect types for required keys

preconditions: raw_response is non-empty
postconditions: ParsedResponseBlock.display_text does not contain the parsed JSON fenced block
ParsedResponseBlock.json_data contains 'ready_to_proceed' key with bool value
ParsedResponseBlock.json_data contains 'problem_model_update' key with dict value
display_text preserves all non-JSON content from the original response
display_text is stripped of leading/trailing whitespace

functions.name: _run_synthesis
functions.description: Execute the synthesis phase. Builds a SynthesisContext from accumulated session state (problem model + full conversation transcript). Delegates artifact generation to the Synthesizer component using a fresh messages array (NOT continued conversation). Parses prompt.md and constraints.yaml from synthesizer output. Presents both artifacts to the engineer via IO. Collects optional feedback. If feedback provided, regenerates ONCE (single retry) with feedback incorporated. Returns the final SynthesisResult.

error_cases.name: synthesis_api_failure
error_cases.condition: API call for synthesis fails after retries

error_cases.name: artifact_parse_failure
error_cases.condition: Cannot parse prompt.md or constraints.yaml from synthesizer output

error_data.message: Failed to parse artifacts from synthesis response

error_cases.name: eof_accept
error_cases.condition: Engineer presses Ctrl+D during feedback prompt, accepting current artifacts

preconditions: UNDERSTAND and CHALLENGE phases are completed
Session has a populated problem model
Session has conversation history from both prior phases
postconditions: SynthesisResult.prompt_md is non-empty valid markdown
SynthesisResult.constraints_yaml is non-empty valid YAML
SynthesisResult.feedback_rounds is 0 or 1
If feedback was provided, artifacts reflect the regeneration
Synthesis used a fresh message array, not the continued conversation
side_effects: Displays generated artifacts to engineer for review
Makes API calls for initial synthesis and optional regeneration

functions.name: _display_resume_summary
functions.description: Display a summary of the resumed session state to the engineer. Shows: session ID, which phases are completed, current phase and round number, key problem model fields discovered so far. Does NOT replay conversation history to the LLM. Called by run_session() when resuming a partially completed session.

inputs.name: session
inputs.description: The loaded Session model to summarize.

preconditions: session is a valid partially-completed Session model
IO is available for display
postconditions: Summary has been displayed via IO.display()
No session state has been modified
No API calls have been made

functions.name: _apply_model_update
functions.description: Apply an incremental update dict to the session's ProblemModel. Merges update keys into the existing model. For list fields, appends new items rather than replacing. For scalar fields, overwrites with new value. Validates that update keys correspond to valid ProblemModel fields; ignores unknown keys with a warning.

inputs.name: update
inputs.description: Incremental update dict from TurnResult.model_update. Keys are ProblemModel field names.

error_cases.name: invalid_update_type
error_cases.condition: Update value type is incompatible with ProblemModel field type

error_data.message: Type mismatch in problem model update

preconditions: update is a dict (may be empty)
Session has an initialized ProblemModel
postconditions: Known scalar fields in ProblemModel are overwritten with update values
Known list fields in ProblemModel have new items appended
Unknown keys in update are ignored (warning displayed via IO)
ProblemModel remains in a valid state after update

structured_side_effects.description: Merges incremental updates into the problem model

functions.name: _build_phase_configs
functions.description: Pure function. Construct PhaseConfig instances from an EngineConfig. Maps understand/challenge round limits to their respective PhaseConfig with appropriate posture_id. Synthesize phase gets min_rounds=1, max_rounds=1 (single pass, feedback handled separately). Validates min <= max for each phase.

error_cases.name: invalid_bounds
error_cases.condition: min_rounds > max_rounds for any phase in config

preconditions: config is a valid EngineConfig
postconditions: Returned dict has keys for all three Phase enum variants
Each value is a valid PhaseConfig
UNDERSTAND PhaseConfig has min/max from config.understand_min/max_rounds
CHALLENGE PhaseConfig has min/max from config.challenge_min/max_rounds
SYNTHESIZE PhaseConfig has min_rounds=1, max_rounds=1
All PhaseConfigs have appropriate posture_id values

invariants: Engine uses a single Claude model (claude-sonnet-4-20250514) for all phases — no model switching
System prompts swap per phase but are NEVER revealed to the engineer in terminal output
Conversation history is cumulative within understand and challenge phases — messages accumulate
Synthesis uses a fresh message array — NOT the continued conversation from prior phases
Full LLM responses (including JSON blocks) are stored in message history; only display_text is shown to engineer
Round enforcement is hard: below min_rounds, ready_to_proceed=true is ignored; at max_rounds, phase transitions regardless
Phase transitions display clear terminal indicators (e.g., '--- Moving to challenge phase ---')
On KeyboardInterrupt, session state is ALWAYS saved before exit
On EOFError in understand/challenge, phase advances to next; in synthesis, current artifacts are accepted
API retries use exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s) and only retry on transient errors (rate limit, timeout, connection)
Parse retries re-prompt the LLM with format correction instructions — they do NOT retry the same prompt
Session resume displays summary but does NOT replay history to the LLM — history is loaded from saved state
Synthesis feedback loop allows at most ONE regeneration cycle
TerminalIO abstraction ensures engine never directly calls print() or input() — all I/O goes through the protocol
All file I/O (session persistence) is delegated to the session module — engine never writes files directly
Engine mutates the Session model in place throughout execution; callers should not hold stale references
